match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 27005: Information security risk management. Methodology for identifying, analyzing, evaluating, and treating information security risks within an ISMS context.
match_nodes.version_disambiguation: ISO 27005 versions differ materially. The 2022 edition (third edition) restructured the methodology to align with ISO 31000 risk management vocabulary and introduced scenario-based risk identification. The 2018 edition used a different asset-based approach. Always specify version.

match_nodes.description: ISO/IEC 29147: Vulnerability disclosure. Methodology for vendors to receive and process vulnerability reports from external researchers.
ISO/IEC 30111: Vulnerability handling processes. Methodology for vendors to investigate, remediate, and publish advisories for reported vulnerabilities. Vendor-side counterpart to ISO 29147.
ISO/IEC 27035: Information security incident management. Multi-part methodology for detecting, reporting, assessing, responding to, and learning from security incidents.
ISO 31000: Risk management — Guidelines. General risk management methodology applicable across domains, including information security. Parent framework referenced by ISO 27005.
ISO/IEC 27037: Digital evidence — Identification, collection, acquisition and preservation. First phase of digital forensics methodology.
ISO/IEC 27041: Investigation assurance. Methodology for ensuring digital forensic investigations are conducted with appropriate quality and reliability.
ISO/IEC 27042: Analysis and interpretation of digital evidence. Methodology for analyzing acquired digital evidence and interpreting findings.
ISO/IEC 27043: Incident investigation principles and processes. Overarching framework connecting the forensic investigation process stages (27037→27041→27042).
ISO/IEC 27006: Requirements for bodies providing audit and certification of ISMS. Methodology for certification bodies conducting ISO 27001 audits.
ISO/IEC 27007: Guidelines for ISMS auditing. Methodology for planning, conducting, and reporting ISMS audits, applicable to internal and external auditors.
ISO/IEC 17021: Conformity assessment — Requirements for bodies providing audit and certification of management systems. General audit methodology (parent of 27006).
